# RAVE Blueprint

## Slide 1 — The Category Signal

### Founder Insight
RAVE wins when coffee is treated as a ritual object, not a commodity.

### So What
The market is crowded with functional claims. RAVE should own the emotional and sensory language of premium routine.

### Source Notes
- ev_deadbeef supports the premium ritual angle.
- ev_cafebabe supports the sensory language and category tension.

## Slide 2 — Audience Tension

### Founder Insight
The audience wants a brand that feels elevated without becoming exclusive or cold.

### So What
The blueprint should balance warmth, taste and design discipline.

### Visual Direction
Natural textures, framed typography, warm shadowing and high-contrast packaging cues.

## Slide 3 — Strategic Promise

### Founder Insight
RAVE should be framed as the coffee that makes everyday routines feel intentional.

### So What
This creates a clearer promise than generic taste claims and can travel across channels.

### Source Notes
- ev_deadbeef anchors the routine and ritual insight.

## Slide 4 — Brand Behaviours

### Founder Insight
The brand should act like a cultural editor with taste, restraint and clarity.

### So What
That gives the creative system a point of view beyond product description.

### Expression Rules
- Use short declarative language.
- Keep layouts spacious and editorial.
- Avoid commodity language.

## Slide 5 — Founder Insight

### Founder Insight
The founders need a system that scales across packaging, retail, social and launch materials.

### So What
The blueprint should support repeatable assets without flattening the brand voice.

### Source Notes
- ev_cafebabe supports the multi-format scaling requirement.

## Slide 6 — Campaign Logic

### Founder Insight
The campaign should make the product feel like a daily upgrade.

### So What
That creates a simple narrative bridge from brand promise to creative output.

### Layout Direction
Use a strong opening statement, one supporting proof panel, then a clear call to action.

## Slide 7 — So What

### So What
The creative system should feel premium, warm and exact.

### Source Notes
- ev_deadbeef
- ev_cafebabe

## Slide 8 — Production Guidance

### Founder Insight
The blueprint must translate into production-ready direction without requiring a redesign later.

### So What
Every slide should be legible as a production instruction, not just an idea.

### Source Notes
- ev_deadbeef

### Visual Direction
- Full-bleed imagery where useful.
- Strong type hierarchy.
- Calm, confident spacing.
